Stop Staying
Silent in the Room.

Group Discussions aren't won by the smartest person. They're won by the one who knows how to communicate under pressure. One hour with the right trainer changes that — permanently.

Day Saturday

Reserve My Seat →
The Real Problem

Most students walk out of a GD
wondering what went wrong.

It wasn't your knowledge. It wasn't your ideas. It was that nobody ever taught you the unwritten rules of group communication — how to enter, how to hold space, how to lead without shouting. This workshop does exactly that.

  • 🤐

    You blank out mid-sentence

    You had a great point — but the pressure killed it before it left your mouth. That's a solvable problem, not a personality trait.

  • 🌊

    Louder voices drown you out

    The room gets chaotic. You wait for a gap that never comes. Evaluators notice who tries — and who doesn't even attempt.

  • ⏱

    You can't think fast enough

    Fast structured thinking under time pressure is a trained skill, not a gift. Most students never practise it — until it's too late.

  • 🎭

    You agree but never lead

    Safe nods and polite agreement keep you invisible. Shortlisting panels look for people who steer conversations — not just join them.

Workshop Agenda

60 minutes. Structured. No filler.

  1. 01

    How GDs Are Actually Evaluated

    Understand the scoring lens — body language, entry quality, frequency vs depth, and the synthesis move most students miss entirely.

  2. 02

    The Art of the Strong Entry

    When to go first, how to open with authority, and the exact phrases that signal a confident communicator from sentence one.

  3. 03

    Rapid Argument Building

    A 3-step mental framework to build structured, relevant arguments on any topic in under 30 seconds — even topics you've never seen before.

  4. 04

    Polite Assertiveness & Interruption

    How to reclaim the floor, redirect weak tangents, and hold your ground without sounding aggressive or desperate.

  5. 05

    The Summariser's Power Move

    Closing a GD is the highest-value skill and the most skipped. Learn to own the last 60 seconds of any group discussion.

  6. 06

    Live Mock Round + Feedback

    Practise everything in a real simulated GD. Receive one precise, personalised note on what to fix before your next actual round.

Walk out with these ▸

  • A go-to entry phrase for any topic
  • Confidence to speak first in a group
  • 3 rapid argument-building frameworks
  • Language to interrupt and reclaim space
  • A personal GD feedback note
  • A mock GD round on your record
Your Trainer

Shambhavi

CSE Student · Soft Skills Trainer · Published Researcher

Shambhavi is a Computer Science Engineering student at Manav Rachna University whose work sits at the precise intersection of technology and human communication. She has trained students, spoken in front of packed conference halls, and won 1st place at IEEE's "Her Lens. Her Voice" for women empowerment — all before her second year of college.

As Marketing Head of FireGuard — an AI fire detection startup that earned Silver at EDII Ahmedabad and 2nd place on National Startup Day — she knows exactly how to make ideas land in high-stakes rooms. She has anchored international conferences, conducted hackathon interviews at HackMor & HackVerse, and published research on soft skills in CS education (IJMRRS, Vol. 8, Issue 1).

As Academic Support Intern at Manav Rachna Educational Institutions, she ran live communication sessions for students and PhD scholars — covering technical writing, confidence building, and presentation skills. She doesn't lecture about communication. She practises it every day.

  • 🏆 1st Place · IEEE "Her Lens. Her Voice" — Women Empowerment
  • 🥈 Silver · EDII Ahmedabad · National Startup Competition
  • 📝 Published · IJMRRS Vol. 8 · Soft Skills in CS Education
  • 🎤 International Conference Anchor & Speaker
  • 📋 Certified: Udemy Soft Skills Coach · TCS iON · AWS · NISM · Cisco
